name: Marco Rubio
subject: Remembering 9/11
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Marco Rubio for President
body:
Rubio-9/11

html, body {background-image: none !important; background-color:
transparent !important; margin:0 !important; padding:0
!important;}

Peter, Today, we pause to remember the 2,977 innocent souls
whom we lost this morning fourteen years ago. On September 11,
2001, I was away from my family, in Tallahassee, a year into
serving as majority whip of the Florida House. Only a month
before, I'd been with my wife Jeanette and our daughter on the
107th floor of the World Trade Center, at Windows on the World.
In just one morning, those buildings we'd just visited, that
shining example of American might, were gone. The horrors of that
day are still fresh in the minds of all Americans, and are a
constant reminder of the evil that always threatens us. Just this
year, we've seen men in uniform killed here on our homeland by
Islamic extremists, cartoonists slaughtered in cold blood in
Paris, and innocent Christians and Muslims beheaded by jihadists
in the Middle East. These events, and the memory of those killed
on 9/11 at the World Trade Center, the Pentagon, and in
Pennsylvania should steel us in our long fight against Islamic
extremism. My thoughts and prayers today, as always, are with the
victims of terrorism. The single bright lining to that awful
September day was the sense of heroism, purpose, and patriotism
that it awakened in many Americans. May we also remember that
feeling, and keep it with us, as we confront America's many
challenges today. Sincerely,Marco Rubio

Paid for by Marco Rubio for President
